mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) above 4% signals rising inflation expectations, leading to a decrease in the purchasing power of consumers and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 4% signals a tightening labor market, leading to increased wage pressures and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening labor markets are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) above 2% signals an expansion in economic activity, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain negative in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a simultaneous increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) above 4% signal a monetary policy mismatch, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in the Real Disposable Personal Income (RSAFS) below 2% signals a decrease in consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the Nonfarm Payroll Employment (PAYEMS) above 200,000 signals a strong labor market, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in strong labor markets are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the Housing Starts (HOUST) above 1,500,000 signals an expansion in the housing market, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain negative in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.
